extraction.one_line_summary: BILBAO Pello took his second stage win by following LANDA Mikel's wheel after CARAPAZ Richard towed them up to chase remnants, while ROGLIČ Primož cracked badly defending third place.
tactical_patterns: LÓPEZ Miguel Ángel attacked twice on Croce d'Aune forcing CARAPAZ to chase personally, unusual for a maglia rosa defender
ROGLIČ Primož pulled entire LANDA/NIBALI/CARAPAZ group up Monte Avena despite being third on GC, catastrophic miscalculation costing 54s
BILBAO sat fourth wheel behind NIBALI in finale despite being strongest sprinter, waited until 150m flat section to strike
CARAPAZ led out LANDA in sprint instead of contesting himself, prioritizing team stage win over personal glory

rider_signals.BILBAO Pello: positive: won sprint against motivated climbers after full stage, replicates week-one strength in finale positioning discipline
rider_signals.LÓPEZ Miguel Ángel: negative: fifth mechanical/crash of Giro, crowd interference on descent plus repeated equipment failures signals organizational chaos
rider_signals.ROGLIČ Primož: negative: did all pulling for LANDA/NIBALI group then cracked for 54s, tactical suicide defending third place
rider_signals.LANDA Mikel: positive: strong enough to contest sprint after 194km/5500m, secured podium by 6-second margin via bonus seconds
rider_signals.CARAPAZ Richard: positive: chased LÓPEZ attacks personally without panic, prioritized team leadout over stage win showing calculated confidence
rider_signals.NIBALI Vincenzo: neutral: stayed with podium group but lacked sprint, no winning moves at 34 years on hardest stage
